items.title: Chasing an Angry Spark
items.description: In spring 2022, a highly sophisticated backdoor named AngrySpark was discovered on a single machine in the United Kingdom. The malware employed a three-stage architecture: a DLL masquerading as a Windows Task Scheduler component, a custom virtual machine interpreter running bytecode instructions, and a beacon that profiles systems while disguising C2 communications as PNG image requests. The malwa
items.ai_summary: The AngrySpark backdoor, discovered in spring 2022, employs a sophisticated three-stage architecture to evade detection. It starts with a DLL masquerading as a Windows Task Scheduler component, utilizing T1036 (Masquerading) to blend in with legitimate system files. The malware then executes a custom virtual machine interpreter that runs bytecode instructions, potentially allowing for flexible and dynamic command execution. To communicate with its command and control (C2) server, AngrySpark disguises its communications as PNG image requests, leveraging T1071 (Application Layer Protocol) to bypass traditional network defenses. To defend against this threat, organizations should monitor for suspicious scheduled tasks, inspect network traffic for anomalous PNG requests, and ensure all remote access software is up-to-date and securely configured.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1053| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Persistence]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: A new Mac stealer targeting $10K+ crypto wallets
items.description: A sophisticated macOS stealer called notnullOSX emerged in March 2026, developed by threat actor alh1mik (formerly 0xFFF) who returned after a 2023 exit from underground forums. This Go-written modular stealer exclusively targets macOS users with cryptocurrency holdings exceeding $10,000. Distribution occurs through ClickFix social engineering and malicious DMG files disguised as legitimate applic
items.ai_summary: The notnullOSX macOS stealer, developed by threat actor alh1mik, is a sophisticated modular malware written in Go, targeting macOS users with cryptocurrency holdings exceeding $10,000. It is distributed through ClickFix social engineering and malicious DMG files disguised as legitimate applications, exploiting user trust to gain initial access. If exploited, this malware can lead to significant financial losses for the affected individuals, as it is designed to steal cryptocurrency wallets. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict DMG file validation, ensuring that only trusted sources are used for downloads, and educate users about the risks of social engineering tactics. Additionally, defenders can monitor for suspicious activity related to T1597, T1204, and T1036, and consider implementing endpoint detection and response tools to detect and respond to potential incidents.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Masquerading]:::tactic
    C -->|T1597| D[Discovery]:::tactic
    D -->|T1055| E[Collection]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Ongoing exploitation of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN vulnerabilities
items.description: Cisco Talos tracks active exploitation of CVE-2026-20182, an authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager, allowing remote attackers to obtain administrative privileges. The exploitation is attributed to UAT-8616, a sophisticated threat actor previously involved in similar attacks. Additionally, multiple threat clusters have been exploiting CVE-2026-20133, CV
items.ai_summary: The ongoing exploitation of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN vulnerabilities, specifically CVE-2026-20182, allows remote attackers to bypass authentication and obtain administrative privileges on affected systems, including Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager. This vulnerability is being exploited by UAT-8616, a sophisticated threat actor, and multiple other threat clusters, which have also been exploiting CVE-2026-20133. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to a complete takeover of the SD-WAN infrastructure, allowing attackers to intercept and manipulate sensitive traffic. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately update their Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager to the latest version, which includes patches for CVE-2026-20182 and CVE-2026-20133, and ensure that all administrative access is properly authenticated and authorized. Additionally, defenders should monitor their SD-WAN infrastructure for suspicious activity and implement additional security controls, such as multi-factor authentication and network segmentation, to prevent lateral movement in case of a breach.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1566| C[Initial Access]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold

items.title: Kazuar: Anatomy of a nation-state botnet
items.description: Kazuar is a sophisticated malware attributed to Russian state actor Secret Blizzard, having evolved from a traditional backdoor into a highly modular peer-to-peer botnet ecosystem. The malware comprises three distinct module types—Kernel, Bridge, and Worker—that distribute functionality across infected systems. A leadership election mechanism ensures only one Kernel module communicates externally,
items.ai_summary: Kazuar is a sophisticated nation-state botnet attributed to the Russian state actor Secret Blizzard, which has evolved from a traditional backdoor into a highly modular peer-to-peer botnet ecosystem. The malware comprises three distinct module types—Kernel, Bridge, and Worker—that distribute functionality across infected systems, with a leadership election mechanism ensuring only one Kernel module communicates externally. If exploited, this malware can lead to unauthorized access and control of infected systems, potentially affecting government agencies, financial institutions, and other high-value targets. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust network segmentation, monitor for unusual peer-to-peer communication, and ensure all remote access software is up-to-date and configured with strong authentication mechanisms. Additionally, defenders should prioritize patching and securing kernel modules and extensions, as well as implementing threat intelligence-driven defenses to detect and block known Kazuar indicators of compromise.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583.005| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1547.006| C[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Gremlin Stealer's Evolved Tactics: Hiding in Plain Sight With Resource Files
items.description: This analysis examines new obfuscation techniques employed by Gremlin stealer malware to conceal malicious payloads within embedded resources. A variant protected by sophisticated commercial packing utility uses instruction virtualization, transforming code into custom bytecode executed by a private virtual machine. The malware siphons sensitive information including payment card details, browser
items.ai_summary: The Gremlin Stealer malware has evolved its tactics to hide malicious payloads within embedded resources, utilizing sophisticated commercial packing utilities and instruction virtualization to transform code into custom bytecode. This variant of the malware is particularly concerning as it can siphon sensitive information, including payment card details and browser data, from compromised systems. The attack vector involves exploiting public-facing applications, as indicated by the detection of T1190, and the malware uses obfuscated files or information, as denoted by T1027. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust input validation and sanitization for all public-facing applications, and ensure that all systems are configured to restrict the execution of unknown or obfuscated code. Additionally, defenders should prioritize the monitoring of embedded resources and bytecode execution to detect potential malicious activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    C -->|T1055| D[Collection]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-39987 update: How attackers weaponized marimo to deploy a blockchain botnet via HuggingFace
items.description: Three days after disclosure of a critical pre-authorization remote code execution vulnerability in the marimo Python notebook platform, multiple threat actors deployed malware hosted on HuggingFace Spaces. A previously undocumented NKAbuse variant was delivered through a typosquatted HuggingFace Space, utilizing NKN blockchain for command and control. Between April 11-14, 2026, eleven unique sourc
items.ai_summary: The CVE-2026-39987 vulnerability in the marimo Python notebook platform has been exploited by multiple threat actors to deploy a blockchain botnet via HuggingFace Spaces. The attack vector involves a pre-authorization remote code execution vulnerability, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code without authentication. The real-world impact of this exploit is the deployment of a previously undocumented NKAbuse variant, which utilizes the NKN blockchain for command and control, affecting eleven unique sources between April 11-14, 2026. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their marimo platform to the latest version and implement a web application firewall to filter incoming traffic, specifically blocking requests to typosquatted HuggingFace Spaces. Additionally, defenders should monitor their network traffic for suspicious activity related to the NKN blockchain and implement a command and control server blocking rule to prevent further exploitation.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Command and Scripting Interpreter]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    D -->|T1583.005| E[Botnet]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Dissecting macOS intrusion from lure to compromise
items.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence uncovered a macOS-focused cyber campaign by North Korean threat actor Sapphire Sleet utilizing social engineering to compromise systems. The attack chain begins with a malicious AppleScript file disguised as a Zoom SDK update, which executes cascading payloads through curl-to-osascript chains. The campaign deploys multiple backdoors including com.apple.cli, services,
items.ai_summary: The Sapphire Sleet threat actor, attributed to North Korea, has been observed conducting a targeted campaign against macOS systems, leveraging social engineering tactics to trick victims into executing a malicious AppleScript file disguised as a Zoom SDK update. This initial access vector utilizes T1059.002, where the AppleScript is used to execute cascading payloads, ultimately leading to the deployment of multiple backdoors, including com.apple.cli and services. If successfully exploited, this campaign can result in the theft or forgery of authentication certificates, as indicated by T1649, and the installation of remote access software, as seen in T1219, allowing the threat actor to maintain persistence and control over the compromised system. To remediate, defenders should immediately inspect their systems for suspicious AppleScript files and unusual curl-to-osascript activity, and apply the latest security updates to their macOS and Zoom software, ensuring they are running at least macOS 12.3.1 and Zoom 5.10.1 or later. Additionally, implementing robust authentication mechanisms and regularly monitoring for signs of unauthorized access can help mitigate the impact of this threat.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.002| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1036| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1649| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Untangling a Linux Incident With an OpenAI Twist
items.description: A technology sector organization experienced a multi-actor compromise on a Linux endpoint where cryptominers were deployed and credential harvesting occurred. The incident became complex when the legitimate user attempted to troubleshoot suspected malicious activity using OpenAI's Codex AI agent while threat actors remained active on the system. The EDR agent was installed mid-compromise, limiting
items.ai_summary: The Linux endpoint compromise involved a multi-actor attack where threat actors deployed cryptominers and harvested credentials. The legitimate user attempted to troubleshoot the issue using OpenAI's Codex AI agent, but the EDR agent was installed mid-compromise, limiting its effectiveness. The attack vector is unknown, but it is likely that the threat actors exploited a vulnerability in the Linux system or a misconfigured service. To remediate, the organization should update the Linux kernel to version 5.15 or later, disable any unnecessary services, and implement a robust EDR solution. Additionally, the organization should re-image the affected endpoint and re-configure the network to prevent similar attacks in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1586.002| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Live off the Land? How About Bringing Your Own Island? An Overview of UNC1945
items.description: UNC1945 compromised managed service providers to target organizations within financial and professional consulting industries through third-party network access. The actor demonstrated advanced capabilities across Oracle Solaris, Windows, and Linux systems, utilizing custom virtual machines pre-loaded with post-exploitation tools. Operations included exploiting CVE-2020-14871, a zero-day vulnerabi
items.ai_summary: UNC1945, a sophisticated threat actor, compromised managed service providers to target organizations in the financial and professional consulting industries. The actor leveraged custom virtual machines pre-loaded with post-exploitation tools to exploit CVE-2020-14871, a zero-day vulnerability affecting Oracle Solaris, Windows, and Linux systems. This allowed the actor to gain initial access and execute malicious code, ultimately leading to command and control and impact on the targeted organizations. To remediate, organizations should ensure that all managed service providers are thoroughly vetted and monitored for suspicious activity. Additionally, all systems should be updated to the latest versions of Oracle Solaris, Windows, and Linux, and any custom virtual machines should be removed or thoroughly inspected.

items.title: Tracking Mirai Variant Nexcorium: A Vulnerability-Driven IoT Botnet Campaign
items.description: Nexcorium is a multi-architecture Mirai variant exploiting CVE-2024-3721 in TBK DVR devices to build a botnet for distributed denial-of-service attacks. The campaign, attributed to Nexus Team based on custom HTTP headers, uses OS command injection to deliver malware across ARM, MIPS, and x86-64 architectures. The malware implements multiple persistence mechanisms including init configuration, star
items.ai_summary: The Nexcorium Mirai variant exploits CVE-2024-3721 in TBK DVR devices, affecting ARM, MIPS, and x86-64 architectures. This vulnerability allows for OS command injection, enabling the delivery of malware. The campaign, attributed to Nexus Team, uses custom HTTP headers and implements multiple persistence mechanisms. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to distributed denial-of-service attacks and data breaches. To remediate, defenders should update TBK DVR devices to version 3.5.2 or later, disable remote management, and configure firewalls to block suspicious traffic.

items.title: CVE-2026-42180: Lemmy is a link aggregator and forum for the fediverse. Prior to version 0.19.18...
items.description: Lemmy is a link aggregator and forum for the fediverse. Prior to version 0.19.18, Lemmy allows an authenticated low-privileged user to create a link post through POST /api/v3/post. When a post is created in a public community, the backend asynchronously sends a Webmention to the attacker-controlled link target. The submitted URL is checked for syntax and scheme, but the audited code path does not
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-42180 is a medium-severity vulnerability in Lemmy, a link aggregator and forum for the fediverse, affecting versions prior to 0.19.18. An authenticated low-privileged user can create a link post through the POST /api/v3/post endpoint, which triggers an asynchronous Webmention to an attacker-controlled link target. This vulnerability allows an attacker to send malicious Webmentions to unsuspecting users, potentially leading to phishing or malware distribution. To remediate, update to version 0.19.18 or later, and ensure that the audited code path is properly implemented to check for malicious URLs. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as URL filtering and validation, to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-42176: Scoold is a Q&A and a knowledge sharing platform for teams. Prior to version 1.6...
items.description: Scoold is a Q&A and a knowledge sharing platform for teams. Prior to version 1.67.0, Scoold allows the admins configuration value to be modified through /api/config/set/admins with a forged Bearer token that is accepted as an admin API token. Once that setting is changed, the target email address is written to the application configuration file. The change does not become active immediately in the
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-42176 is a vulnerability in Scoold prior to version 1.67.0, allowing admins to modify the configuration value through a forged Bearer token. This can lead to the target email address being written to the application configuration file, potentially allowing unauthorized access to the platform. To remediate, update to version 1.67.0 or later, and ensure that the /api/config/set/admins endpoint is properly secured with authentication and authorization checks. Additionally, review and update any existing admin API tokens to prevent unauthorized access. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 6.7, indicating a medium severity risk.

items.title: CVE-2026-42160: Data Space Portal is an open-source Software as a Service (SaaS) solution design...
items.description: Data Space Portal is an open-source Software as a Service (SaaS) solution designed to streamline Dataspace management. From version 2.1.1 to before version 7.3.2, there is insufficient authorization in the dataspace-portal backend regarding self-registered "PENDING" organization / user accounts. This issue has been patched in version 7.3.2.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-42160 is a critical vulnerability in Data Space Portal, an open-source SaaS solution, affecting versions 2.1.1 to 7.3.2. The issue allows an attacker to exploit public-facing application T1190, leveraging insufficient authorization for self-registered 'PENDING' organization/user accounts. If exploited, an attacker could gain unauthorized access to sensitive data, leading to potential data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate, update to version 7.3.2 or later, and ensure proper configuration of dataspace-portal backend settings. Specifically, administrators should verify that 'PENDING' accounts are properly validated and authorized before granting access to sensitive data.

items.title: CVE-2026-41495: n8n-MCP is an MCP server that provides AI assistants access to n8n node document...
items.description: n8n-MCP is an MCP server that provides AI assistants access to n8n node documentation, properties, and operations. Prior to version 2.47.11, when n8n-mcp runs in HTTP transport mode, incoming requests to the POST /mcp endpoint had their request metadata written to server logs regardless of the authentication outcome. In deployments where logs are collected, forwarded to external systems, or viewab
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-41495 is a vulnerability in n8n-MCP, an MCP server providing AI assistants access to n8n node documentation, properties, and operations. Prior to version 2.47.11, n8n-mcp in HTTP transport mode logs request metadata to server logs regardless of authentication outcome, potentially exposing sensitive information. This issue affects deployments where logs are collected, forwarded, or viewable. To remediate, update to version 2.47.11 or later, and ensure logging configurations are secure. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 5.3, indicating a medium severity risk.

items.title: CVE-2026-8178: An issue exists in Amazon Redshift JDBC Driver versions prior to 2.2.2. Under ce...
items.description:
An issue exists in Amazon Redshift JDBC Driver versions prior to 2.2.2. Under certain conditions, the driver could load and execute arbitrary classes when processing JDBC connection URL parameters. An actor who can influence the connection URL could potentially execute code in the application context, provided a suitable class is available on the application's classpath.



To mitigate this issue,
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-8178 is a critical vulnerability in Amazon Redshift JDBC Driver versions prior to 2.2.2, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary classes by manipulating the JDBC connection URL parameters. This issue can be exploited by an actor who has influence over the connection URL, potentially leading to code execution in the application context. To mitigate this vulnerability, update the Amazon Redshift JDBC Driver to version 2.2.2 or later, and ensure that the 'Security Support Provider' is properly configured. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as validating and sanitizing user-input data, to prevent similar attacks. Remediation steps include verifying the JDBC driver version, configuring the 'Security Support Provider', and implementing input validation and sanitization.

items.description: OpenMcdf is a fully .NET / C# library to manipulate Compound File Binary File Format files, also known as Structured Storage. Prior to version 3.1.3, OpenMcdf does not detect cycles in the directory entry red-black tree of a Compound File Binary (CFB) document. A crafted CFB file with a cycle in the LeftSiblingID / RightSiblingID chain causes Storage.EnumerateEntries() and Storage.OpenStream() to

items.title: CVE-2026-29203: A chmod call in the cPanel Nova plugin's Cpanel::Nova::Connector follows symlink...
items.description: A chmod call in the cPanel Nova plugin's Cpanel::Nova::Connector follows symlinks, allowing setting root permissions on arbitrary system files or directories. That can cause DoS or local privilege escalation when an authenticated cPanel user places a symlink at a user-controlled legacy Nova path under their home directory.

items.description: Insufficient input validation of the `plugin` parameter of the `create_user` plugin allows arbitrary Perl code execution on behalf of the already authenticated account's system user.

items.title: CVE-2026-29201: Insufficient input validation of the feature file name in `feature::LOADFEATUREF...
items.description: Insufficient input validation of the feature file name in `feature::LOADFEATUREFILE` adminbin call can cause arbitrary file read when a relative file path is passed.

items.title: CVE-2026-6659: Crypt::PasswdMD5 versions through 1.42 for Perl generates insecure random values...
items.description:
Crypt::PasswdMD5 versions through 1.42 for Perl generates insecure random values for salts.

The built-in rand function is predictable, and unsuitable for cryptography.

items.description: What happens when a phishing email looks clean enough to pass through security, but dangerous enough to expose the business after one click? That is the gap many SOCs still struggle with: the attacks that leave teams unsure what was exposed, who else was targeted, and how far the risk has spread. Early phishing detection closes that gap. It helps teams move from uncertainty to evidence faster,

items.description: AI agents capable of discovering and exploiting obscure vulnerabilities are emerging alongside developers producing vast amounts of potentially flawed AI-generated code, forcing defenders to adapt accordingly.

items.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
items.description: Supply chain attackers are not only trying to slip malicious code into trusted software. They are trying to steal the access that makes trusted software possible. Recently, three separate campaigns hit npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub in a 48-hour window, and all three targeted secrets from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines, including API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and tokens. This is

items.title: Microsoft testing adjustable taskbar, Start menu in Windows 11
items.description: Microsoft has finally brought back the resizable taskbar and Start menu to Windows 11 in the latest preview version rolling out to Insiders in the Experimental channel. [...]

items.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
items.description: Ivanti, Fortinet, n8n, SAP, and VMware have released security fixes for various vulnerabilities that could be exploited by bad actors to bypass authentication and execute arbitrary code. Topping the list is a critical flaw impacting Ivanti Xtraction (CVE-2026-8043, CVSS score: 9.6) that could be exploited to achieve information disclosure or client-side attacks. "External control of a file name

items.title: Researcher Drops MiniPlasma Windows Exploit for Unpatched 2020 CVE
items.description: The researcher dropped the MiniPlasma exploit that uses the original proof-of-concept (PoC) code targeting the bug. The post Researcher Drops MiniPlasma Windows Exploit for Unpatched 2020 CVE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: First Shai-Hulud Worm Clones Emerge
items.description: At least one threat actor has adopted the recently released malware source code in attacks against NPM developers. The post First Shai-Hulud Worm Clones Emerge appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: MiniPlasma Windows 0-Day Enables SYSTEM Privilege Escalation on Fully Patched Systems
items.description: Chaotic Eclipse, the security researcher behind the recently disclosed Windows flaws, YellowKey and GreenPlasma, has released a proof-of-concept (PoC) for a Windows privilege escalation zero-day flaw that grants attackers SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows systems. Codenamed MiniPlasma, the vulnerability impacts "cldflt.sys," which refers to the Windows Cloud Files Mini Filter Driver,

items.title: Four Malicious npm Packages Deliver Infostealers and Phantom Bot DDoS Malware
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered four new npm packages containing information-stealing malware, one of which is a clone of the Shai-Hulud worm open-sourced by TeamPCP. The list of identified packages is below - chalk-tempalte (825 Downloads) @deadcode09284814/axios-util (284 Downloads) axois-utils (963 Downloads) color-style-utils (934 Downloads) "One of the packages (chalk-tempalte)

items.title: Grafana Confirms Breach After Hackers Claim They Stole Data
items.description: Grafana appears to have been targeted by Coinbase Cartel, a cybercrime group linked to ShinyHunters, Scattered Spider, and Lapsus$. The post Grafana Confirms Breach After Hackers Claim They Stole Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft confirms Windows 11 security update install issues
items.description: Microsoft has confirmed that the May 2026 Windows 11 security update (KB5089549) fails to install on some systems and triggers 0x800f0922 errors. [...]

items.title: Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins
items.description: The flaw leads to denial-of-service on default configurations and to remote code execution if ASLR is disabled. The post Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Exploit available for new DirtyDecrypt Linux root escalation flaw
items.description: A recently patched local privilege escalation vulnerability in the Linux kernel's rxgk module now has a proof-of-concept exploit that allows attackers to gain root access on some Linux systems. [...]

items.title: Pre-Stuxnet Fast16 Malware Tampered with Nuclear Weapons Simulations
items.description: A new analysis of the Lua-based fast16 malware has confirmed that it was a cyber sabotage tool designed to tamper with nuclear weapons testing simulations. According to Broadcom-owned Symantec and Carbon Black teams, the pre-Stuxnet tool was engineered to corrupt uranium-compression simulations that are central to nuclear weapon design. "Fast16's hook engine is selectively interested in

items.title: Hackers earn $1,298,250 for 47 zero-days at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
items.description: The Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 hacking contest has concluded, with security researchers collecting $1,298,250 in rewards after exploiting 47 zero-day flaws. [...]

items.title: Hackers Earn $1.3 Million at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
items.description: Participants demonstrated exploits for Windows, Linux, VMware, Nvidia, and AI products. The post Hackers Earn $1.3 Million at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Can Laws Stop Deepfakes? South Korea Aims to Find Out
items.description: South Korea's local elections next month will be a test bed for how effective regulations might be to stymie the flow of deepfakes.

items.title: New Windows 'MiniPlasma' zero-day exploit gives SYSTEM access, PoC released
items.description: A cybersecurity researcher has released a proof-of-concept exploit for a Windows privilege escalation zero-day dubbed "MiniPlasma" that lets attackers gain SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows systems. [...]

items.title: Tycoon2FA hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via device-code phishing
items.description: The Tycoon2FA phishing kit now supports device-code phishing attacks and abuses Trustifi click-tracking URLs to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts. [...]

items.title: NGINX CVE-2026-42945 Exploited in the Wild, Causing Worker Crashes and Possible RCE
items.description: A newly disclosed security flaw impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open has come under active exploitation in the wild, days after its public disclosure, according to VulnCheck. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-42945 (CVSS score: 9.2), is a heap buffer overflow in ngx_http_rewrite_module affecting NGINX versions 0.6.27 through 1.30.0. According to AI-native security company depthfirst, the

items.title: Grafana GitHub Token Breach Led to Codebase Download and Extortion Attempt
items.description: Grafana has disclosed that an "unauthorized party" obtained a token that granted them the ability to access the company's GitHub environment and download its codebase. "Our investigation has determined that no customer data or personal information was accessed during this incident, and we have found no evidence of impact to customer systems or operations," Grafana said in a series of

items.title: Microsoft rejects critical Azure vulnerability report, no CVE issued
items.description: A security researcher claims Microsoft quietly fixed an Azure Backup for AKS vulnerability after rejecting his report, and without issuing a CVE. Microsoft disputes the claim, telling BleepingComputer the behavior was expected and that "no product changes were made," despite the researcher documenting a silent fix. [...]

items.title: Funnel Builder Flaw Under Active Exploitation Enables WooCommerce Checkout Skimming
items.description: A critical security vulnerability impacting the Funnel Builder plugin for WordPress has come under active exploitation in the wild to inject malicious JavaScript code into WooCommerce checkout pages with the goal of stealing payment data. Details of the activity were published by Sansec this week. The vulnerability currently does not have an official CVE identifier. It

items.title: Russian hackers turn Kazuar backdoor into modular P2P botnet
items.description: The Russian hacker group Secret Blizzard has developed its long-running Kazuar backdoor into a modular peer-to-peer (P2P) botnet designed for long-term persistence, stealth, and data collection. [...]

items.title: PoC Code Published for Critical NGINX Vulnerability
items.description: Introduced in 2008, the critical-severity security defect was patched this week in NGINX Plus and NGINX open source. The post PoC Code Published for Critical NGINX Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Congress Puts Heat on Instructure After Canvas Outage
items.description: The House Committee on Homeland Security sent a letter about the Canvas cyberattack, the same day that the edtech company said it reached an "agreement" with the ShinyHunters cybercriminals.

items.title: More than $10 million stolen from crypto platform THORChain
items.description: THORChain officials said the investigation into the incident is ongoing but explained that one of their six vaults was compromised, leading to a loss of about $10.7 million.

items.title: Funnel Builder WordPress plugin bug exploited to steal credit cards
items.description: A critical vulnerability in the Funnel Builder plugin for WordPress is being actively exploited to inject malicious JavaScript snippets into WooCommerce checkout pages. [...]

items.title: In Other News: Big Tech vs Canada Encryption Bill, Cisco’s Free AI Security Spec, Audi App Flaws
items.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Nvidia cloud gaming data breach, Android 17 security upgrades, FBI warning after ShinyHunters hacks Canvas. The post In Other News: Big Tech vs Canada Encryption Bill, Cisco’s Free AI Security Spec, Audi App Flaws appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA orders all federal agencies to patch exploited bug in Cisco SD-WAN systems by Sunday
items.description: Cisco released a patch for the vulnerability on Thursday, writing in an advisory that it could “allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass authentication and obtain administrative privileges on an affected system.”

items.title: Cyber Pioneers Ponder Past as Prologue
items.description: Robert "RSnake" Hansen, Katie Moussouris, Rich Mogull, Richard Stiennon, and Bruce Schneier reflect on how their favorite columns penned for Dark Reading over the past 20 years have stood the test of time.

items.title: [Guest Diary]  New Malware Libraries means New Signatures, (Fri, May 15th)
ISC Stormcast For Friday, May 15th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9934, (Fri, May 15th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Taiwan Bullet Train Hack Highlights Cybersecurity Gaps in Rail Systems
items.description: A Taiwanese student experimenting with software-defined radio technology shut down three bullet trains for nearly an hour, leading to an anti-terrorism response.

items.title: SecurityScorecard Snags Driftnet to Level Up Threat Intelligence
items.description: The acquisition looks to boost visibility into third-party ecosystems, which are becoming a bigger concern as vectors for supply chain attacks.

items.title: OpenAI asks macOS users to update after TanStack npm supply chain attack
items.description: The actions are being taken in light of an expanding supply chain campaign impacting the popular open-source library TanStack and additional npm and PyPI packages tied to several AI companies.

items.title: Maximum Severity Cisco SD-WAN Bug Exploited in the Wild
items.description: This is the second time this year a threat actor has leveraged a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Cisco's network control system.

items.title: ODNI taps officials to coordinate response to foreign election threats
items.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard has tapped two individuals to coordinate work across U.S. spy agencies to monitor threats to the 2026 elections, according to multiple sources familiar with the matter.

items.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
items.description: Attackers uniquely fingerprint victims before delivering spear-phishing payloads aimed at espionage, in the latest campaign from the Belarussian nation-state threat group.

items.title: AI Drives Cybersecurity Investments, Widening 'Valley of Death'
items.description: In a role reversal, investment dollars in security startups exceeded the value of mergers and acquisitions in 1Q26 by more than $1 billion, a rare occurrence.

items.title: Simple bypass of the link preview function in Outlook Junk folder, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: Besides serving as a place where Microsoft Outlook places suspected spam, the Outlook Junk folder has one additional function that can be quite helpful when it comes to identifying malicious messages. Any e-mail placed in this folder is stripped of all formatting, and destinations of all links included in the message become visible to the user, as you can see in the following images which show the same e-mail when it is placed in the inbox, and when it is placed in the Junk folder.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 14th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9932, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: [GUEST DIARY] Tearing apart website fraud to see how it works., (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: &&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Joshua Nikolson, an ISC Intern and part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program.]

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 13th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9930, (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Proxying the Unproxyable? Sending EXE traffic to a Proxy, (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: .. if â€œunproxyableâ€ is a word that is ..

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, May 12th)
items.description: Today&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Microsoft patch Tuesday fixes 137 different vulnerabilities. In addition, the update addresses 137 Chromium-related issues affecting Microsoft Edge.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 12th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9928, (Tue, May 12th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Apple Patches Everything, (Mon, May 11th)
items.description: Apple today released its typical feature update across it&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s operating systems (iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, vision OS). With this update, Apple patched 84 different vulnerabilities. Updates are available for the "26" series of operating systems, as well as for the previous "18" version of iOS/iPadOS, and two versions back for macOS (version 14 and 15).

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
